methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Nord Quantique is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised C$40M+ in total funding, including a C$23M commitment from the Quantum Champions fund in December 2025. The target operates within a quantum computing sector represented by a comp universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable to Nord Quantique due to an insufficient narrow peer set. While 7 same-sector quantum computing comparables exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than 3 datapoints—the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of early-stage technology valuations and the specialized nature of quantum computing development.

Without a sufficiently filtered peer cohort or triangulated valuation range derived from standard methods, the analysis cannot support a specific valuation point or range at this time. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrowly comparable seed-stage quantum companies with disclosed valuations and the extreme variance in quantum computing business models and capital efficiency metrics across the existing comp set. Additional data on recent seed and Series A rounds within quantum computing, or on Nord Quantique's specific technical differentiation and burn rate, would be required to conduct a defensible valuation.
